relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: When the earthly tent is taken down, a Divine-made dwelling remains.
The soul groans to be clothed with heavenly dwelling.
The longing is not nakedness but true clothing.
The mortal is not despised; it is swallowed by life.
Spirit is the pledge of this life-swallowing future.
Courage remains while living in the body.
We walk through trust, not sight.
Courage also longs for home with the Lord.
The aim in every state is to be pleasing to him.
Life through the body matters before Messiah’s judgment seat.
Reverence of the Lord gives urgency and transparency.
Paul contrasts heart-reality with face-performance.
Ecstasy is for the Divine; sober-mindedness is for the community.
Messiah-love holds us together.
The living no longer live for themselves but for the raised One.
Flesh-level knowing is no longer the measure.
In Messiah: new creation; old things pass, new things come.
All this is from the Divine who reconciles and gives reconciliation-service.
The Divine in Messiah reconciles the world, not counting trespasses.
Ambassadors carry the Divine appeal: be reconciled.
The sinless one enters the sin-place so we become Divine justice in him.

contemplative_note: 2 Corinthians 5 is reconciliation medicine. The mortal is swallowed by life, the world is reconciled in Messiah, and the community becomes an ambassador-field of new creation.
